observations.recommendation:
Management to:
Instruct the accounting and general services department to reconcile PPELCs and Property Cards maintained, respectively;
Direct the inventory committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all the municipality’s properties and to reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records; and
Direct the GSO/disposal committee to undertake immediate action to properly dispose of all of damaged, obsolete, unserviceable or idle properties to prevent them from further deterioration.

Management to prepare and submit semestral inventory reports; Utilize the RIS in the issuance of materials and prepare the SSMI as a basis of the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV; and Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer – Designate to maintain SLC and Stock Cards, respectively, and reconcile the same monthly.

Management to direct the accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers and preparation of the financial statements for these accounts to ensure adequate information as to the assets, liabilities and equity of each special account especially for monitoring purposes.
File the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and long outstanding unliquidated cash advances that could no longer be collected pursuant to the abovementioned regulation.

The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to properly effect the necessary adjustments and make sure that reported year-end cash balances per book are always reconciled with that reported by the bank. Furthermore, management should exert extra efforts to identify unreconciled book and bank balances as of December 2008 totaling negative P2.379 million which remained unrecorded as of December 31, 2017.

The Municipality to consider the following: Review personnel requirement of Treasurer’s Office to implement the code including the provision on License Inspector; Device the sworn statement form to facilitate compliance of the taxpayer on the declaration of revenue in obtaining business permit and residence certificate. Review certain provisions especially on Cart or Sledge Registration Fee; Dog License Fee; Permit Fee on Agricultural Machinery and Other Heavy Equipment; Permit fee for the Conduct of Group Activities; Fighting Cock License Fee; Permit fee on Profession not requiring Government Examination; and Mayor’s Clearance to transport and ship various animals and goods; Adopt proper and clear procedure on how to implement certain provision of the code; Review the policy on slaughter fee in the absence of a Municipal Slaughterhouse.
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the accounts and make the necessary corrections to have an accurate balance on its liability account.

We recommended the LCE to:
Create and Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members with at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Division/Units of the Agency who is mandated to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the specific duties and assignment of its members from start up to its targeted final completion date to be able to come up with the required report on the physical inventory;
Relieve the personnel who were designated as Inventory Committee members of their present and regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is finally completed and submitted their output/reports following the specific guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.
Submit the inventory report on or before June 30, 2022.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MA:
To make reconciliation on the difference of the loan balance between the amount presented in the FS and the loan amortization schedule provided by the bank where the loan was entered into;
To present the current portion of long-term liabilities under the line-item Financial Liabilities in the SFP; and
To make disclosure in the NTFS the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity on information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. one.

We recommended that the MA should make reconciliation on the liability accounts with negative (debit) balances so the financial statement reports be more reflective of the actual financial position of the agency as of the reporting period.
We recommended that the MA prepare a JEV in the GF to record the equipment as follows:
We recommended that management direct the MA and MT to conduct the required reconciliation of their records and investigate such variance noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan taking into consideration whether available manpower has the capability to implement on time the PPAs included in the yearly AIP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the completion and submission of the PSPC for his approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution for its institutionalization.
We also recommended that Management should improve their monitoring of operational requirements like the submission of PSCP to its overseeing agency.
We recommended that the MT to ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for DRRM-MOOE and QRF are transferred to the TF at year-end in conformity with the above cited provisions.

We recommended that the LGU capacitate its GFPS on how to undertake gender analysis by sending them to training seminars and require the creation and maintenance of GAD database containing sex disaggregated data and other gender-related information which shall serve as basis in conducting gender analysis, identifying gender issues and determining the appropriate PAPs that would address the identified gender issues to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that the GFPS include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF) and Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL) Assessment Tool to capacitate themselves in the conduct of gender audit. Further, we recommend that the GFPS conduct gender audit on its agency thru the use of GMEF and GeRL Assessment Tool.
We recommended that the Committee on Women and Family with the assistance of the GFPS update the existing GAD Code to include mandates of RA 9710 and other policies implementing gender equality and empowerment.
We recommended that GFPS administer HGDG tool particularly the PIMME and/or FIMME checklist on all of the major programs/projects attributed to GAD in the GPB to determine the actual expenditures of the major PPAs to be attributed to GAD during the preparation of the GAD AR.

The following to the management:
Municipal Accountant

To adjust the remaining items of the relief
goods as Inventory as:
Dr. Drugs and Medicines
for Distribution  912, 898.80
Cr. Prior Period
Adjustments 912,898.80

To record purchases under the Inventory
account on the basis of the Acceptance and
Inspection Report (AIR) and other supporting
documents such as Purchase Orders (POs),
Delivery Receipts and/or Supplier’s Invoice.

To record expenses on the basis of the Report
of Supplies and
Materials Issued (RSMI)
together with the
supporting RIS.

B.  General Services
Officer

To consolidate the RIS
in the Summary of
Supplies and Materials
Issued and submit the
same to the Office of
the Municipal Accountant
for proper recording of
its issuances in order
to reflect the correct
Inventory and Expense
balances.

The Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry, to take-up an inventory account. Likewise, to ensure recognition of liability account nearing the closing date, proper coordination between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer should be established.

The management:
Local Chief Executive:
Require the department heads to submit RPCPPE per office to fast tract the physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU.
Members of the Inventory Committee:
After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office has to conduct reconciliation to establish the existence, status or conditions, and the whereabouts of the assets; and
Moreover, those missing PPEs established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:
To maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.
Management to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct the required reconciliation of their records and investigate such variance noted.

Local Chief Executive to closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan taking into consideration whether available manpower has the capability to implement on time the PPAs included in the yearly AIP.
Municipal Accountant to refrain from the practice of direct recording of an Asset account and recognize the same as Construction in Progress when the project is not yet completed.
The LCE is enjoined to direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of splitting and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all procurement activities.

The LSB has to convene at least once a month or as often as the chairman or majority of the members convene the same to properly and timely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities funded by the SEF.

The Municipal Accountant to provide firefighting equipment not only limited to fire extinguishers but also fire alarm system at the conspicuous places and formulate post emergency evacuation plan where anyone can have access to mitigate and prevent fire. Likewise, coordinate with the Municipal Fire Marshal for other preventive measures to secure government properties and the lives of its employees when disaster occur.

The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for DRRM-MOOE and Quick Response Fund are transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end in conformity with the above cited provisions.

The management to consider the inclusion in its budget the purchase of equipment capable of handling non-biodegradable waste materials to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies, and to initiate reconciliation of balances with reported RPT/SET Receivables; The Municipal Accountant to reflect the proper adjustments in the General Fund books on the difference noted in the RPT Receivables and Deferred RPT accounts; The Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate regularly to determine the causes of the material discrepancy and to effect possible adjustments, if necessary.

Management to direct the offices concerned to closely monitor project implementation and accomplishments as a means of ensuring that the municipality would attain socio-economic outcomes for its constituents.

The Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law and conduct information and education campaign on real property tax payment. Likewise, initiate measures to increase collection by implementing civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.
Management to fully and strictly impose all provisions of its municipal revenue code.

The GSO to comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulations on fuel consumption and submit the report to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, the process of documentation of fuel utilization be reviewed and make any changes to improve the system in order to facilitate the preparation of the monthly fuel consumption report. Further, all personnel concerned, including all drivers of the LGU to be aware of the proper procedure and the necessity of having a timely and regular report of fuel consumption. Non-compliance with said regulations may cause the suspension and/or disallowance of the voucher for the payment of gas/fuel for the LGU’s motor vehicles and the withholding of salary of personnel concerned until the required reports shall have been submitted.

1.12	We recommended the following to the management:
A.	Municipal Accountant
1.	To adjust the remaining items of the relief goods as Inventory as:
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution		912, 898.80
Prior Period Adjustments				912,898.80
2.	To record purchases under the Inventory account on the basis of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) and other supporting documents such as Purchase Orders (POs), Delivery Receipts and/or Supplier’s Invoice.
3.	To record expenses on the basis of the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) together with the supporting RIS.
B.	General Services Officer
1.	To consolidate the RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant for proper recording of its issuances in order to reflect the correct Inventory and Expense balances.

2.6 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry, to take-up an inventory account. Likewise, to ensure recognition of liability account nearing the closing date, proper coordination between the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer should be established.

3.9 	We recommended the following to the management:
Local Chief Executive:
Require the department heads to submit RPCPPE per office to fast tract the physical inventory taking of properties owned by the LGU.
Members of the Inventory Committee:
1.	After the updated RPCPPE have been made in accordance with the prescribed format, the Accounting and Property/Treasurer’s Office has to conduct reconciliation to establish the existence, status or conditions, and the whereabouts of the assets; and
2.	Moreover, those missing PPEs established during the physical count, demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant:
To maintain Property Card and Equipment Ledger Cards, respectively, for easy determination identification and reconciliation of records.

5.13	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015:
Inventory Committee
Conduct and inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;
Prepare the Report on Physical Count of the Road Network System of the LGU (Annex C of the Circular)
Municipal GSO-Designate
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)
Municipal Engineer
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects
Municipal Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex D of the Circular), respectively;
Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.
6.13	We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct the required reconciliation of their records and investigate such variance noted.

7.6 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive closely monitor the implementation of the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan taking into consideration whether available manpower has the capability to implement on time the PPAs included in the yearly AIP.

8.4 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from the practice of direct recording of an Asset account and recognize the same as Construction in Progress when the project is not yet completed.
We recommended that the LCE direct the officials concerned to stop the practice of splitting and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all procurement activities.

10.6	We recommended that the LSB has to convene at least once a month or as often as the chairman or majority of the members convene the same to properly and timely monitor the implementation of the programs, projects and activities funded by the SEF.

11.14	We recommended that management provide firefighting equipment not only limited to fire extinguishers but also fire alarm system at the conspicuous places and formulate post emergency evacuation plan where anyone can have access to mitigate and prevent fire.

Likewise, coordinate with the Municipal Fire Marshal for other preventive measures to secure government properties and the lives of its employees when disaster occur.

12.11	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the unexpended/unobligated balances for DRRM-MOOE and Quick Response Fund are transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end in conformity with the above cited provisions.

13.12	We recommended that management to consider the inclusion in its budget the purchase of equipment capable of handling non-biodegradable waste materials to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety.
File the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables and long outstanding unliquidated cash advances that could no longer be collected pursuant to the abovementioned regulation.

Instruct the accounting and general services department to reconcile PPELCs and Property Cards maintained, respectively.
	
Moreover, we reiterated management to direct the inventory committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all the municipality’s properties and to reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records.

In addition thereto, the GSO/disposal committee has to undertake immediate action to properly dispose of all of damaged, obsolete, unserviceable or idle properties to prevent them from further deterioration.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly report of water bills to the Municipal Accountant summarizing the water bills sent to consumer which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of waterworks system fees.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply accrual basis in recording the monthly report of water bills as required under Par. 7, PPSAS 1.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly report of bills rendered to market occupant which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of rental income.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply accrual basis in recording the monthly report of water bills as required under Par. 7, PPSAS 1.

The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to properly effect the necessary adjustments and make sure that reported year-end cash balances per book are always reconciled with that reported by the bank.

Furthermore, management should exert extra efforts to identify unreconciled book and bank balances as of December 2008 totaling negative P2,378,555.13 which remained unrecorded as of December 31, 2017.

In consideration of the foregoing observations, we recommend the following:

Management to designate a market administrator from among the personnel in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer with the primary responsibility of making sure that all laws, rules and regulations pertaining to the operation, maintenance and upkeep of the municipal public market be strictly followed to avoid further problems or violations of the revenue ordinance; and

2. For management to fully and strictly impose all provisions of its municipal revenue code.

We recommend that the GSO to comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulations on fuel consumption and submit the report to the Auditor for verification.  

Likewise, the process of documentation of fuel utilization be reviewed and make any changes to improve the system in order to facilitate the preparation of the monthly fuel consumption report.

Further, all personnel concerned, including all drivers of the LGU to be aware of the proper procedure and the necessity of having a timely and regular report of fuel consumption. 

Non-compliance with said regulations may cause the suspension and/or disallowance of the voucher for the payment of gas/fuel for the LGU’s motor vehicles and the withholding of salary of personnel concerned until the required reports shall have been submitted.
Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the accounts and make the necessary corrections to have an accurate balance on its liability account.

1.8 	We recommended management to:
a. 	instruct the accounting and general services department to reconcile PPELCs and Property Cards maintained, respectively;
b. 	direct the inventory committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all the municipality’s properties and to reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records; and
c. 	direct the GSO/disposal committee to undertake immediate action to properly dispose of all of damaged, obsolete, unserviceable or idle properties to prevent them from further deterioration.

2.9 	We recommended:
a. 	the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies, and to initiate reconciliation of balances with reported RPT/SET Receivables;
b. 	the Municipal Accountant to reflect the proper adjustments in the General Fund books on the difference noted in the RPT Receivables and Deferred RPT accounts; and
c.  the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate regularly to determine the causes of the material discrepancy and to effect possible adjustments, if necessary.

3.10	In consideration of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:
a. 	Management to prepare and submit semestral inventory reports;
b. 	Utilized the RIS in the issuance of materials and prepare the SSMI as a basis of the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV; and
c. 	Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer - Designate to maintain SLC and Stock Cards, respectively, and reconcile the same monthly.

4.9	We recommended that management direct the offices concerned to closely monitor project implementation and accomplishments as a means of ensuring that the municipality would attain socio-economic outcomes for its constituents.

5.10 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law and conduct information and education campaign on real property tax payment. Likewise, initiate measures to increase collection by implementing civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
a. 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the monthly report of bills rendered for market occupants and water concessionaires and submit to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the accrual and recording thereof; and
b. 	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply accrual basis upon receipt of the monthly report of bills rendered, as required under Par. 7, PPSAS 1.

7.8 	We recommend that management directs the accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers and preparation of the financial statements for these accounts to ensure adequate information as to the assets, liabilities and equity of each special account especially for monitoring purposes.

Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the Municipality as directed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all assets of the Municipality.

Likewise, we enjoin all concerned, the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to coordinate and work together immediately to attain the objectives of the Circular.

In consideration of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following: 

Recommend the blacklisting of the contractor based on the provisions of Appendix 4 of RA 9184 otherwise known as the “Government Procurement Reform Act”;

To terminate the contract; and

To prepare separate POW for the remaining work to be done for rebidding or to undertake the remaining work by administration.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person strictly comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001.

Conduct capacity development activity for HGDG as a GAD activity in order to determine the score and the corresponding percentage of budget for the year of the regular/mainstreamed PPAs that could be attributed to the GAD budget.

We recommended management to instruct the accounting and general services department to reconcile PPELCs and Property Cards maintained, respectively.

Moreover, we reiterated management to direct the inventory committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all the municipality’s properties and to reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records.

In addition thereto, the GSO/disposal committee has to undertake immediate action to properly dispose of all of damaged, obsolete, unserviceable or idle properties to prevent them from further deterioration.

Likewise, we enjoin all concerned, the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to coordinate and work together immediately to attain the objectives of the Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly report of water bills to the Municipal Accountant summarizing the water bills sent to consumer which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of waterworks system fees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

In consideration of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:
Recommend the blacklisting of the contractor based on the provisions of Appendix 4 of RA 9184 otherwise known as the “Government Procurement Reform Act”;
2.  To terminate the contract; and
3. To prepare separate POW for the remaining work to be done for rebidding or to undertake the remaining work by administration.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person strictly comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001.

Moreover, we recommended the municipality to conduct capacity development activity for HGDG as a GAD activity in order to determine the score and the corresponding percentage of budget for the year of the regular/mainstreamed PPAs that could be attributed to the GAD budget.

observations.managementAction: Based on the reconciliation made by working it back, management found out that discrepancies and errors were due to erroneous recording of account code entries. Further verification shows that some accumulated amount were from previous records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant which was forwarded but left unreconciled. Necessary corrections were made to some accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant cannot reconcile the said items for some of the records were lost.

observations.managementAction:
Accordingly, the negative balance of account honoraria was due to the failure of the Municipal Treasurer to remit the collection of sale of bidding documents.

Based on the cash count made last December 2013, two (2) stubs of official receipt were not remitted.

The report of collections was made not in accordance of what is provided in Sec. 15 and 29 of NGAS due to lack of internal control of the Treasurer or Head of the Agency. The collectors remitted their collections, but the corresponding accountable forms were not properly declared. Only the total amount collected was reflected as collections and deposits, thus accumulating the amount of cash in vault to a bigger amount.

observations.reason:
The accountable 
Officers did not remit their collections daily. On the other hand, the liquidation of cash advances per verification was complied with.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to properly effect the necessary adjustments and make sure that reported year-end cash balances per book should always reconcile with that reported by the bank.
On the other hand, charges to the cash accounts which are considered indirect disbursements should be properly covered with Obligation Requests before this can be taken up in the books to ensure that disbursements are properly budgeted. Thus, corresponding sufficient appropriation to cover all regular bank charges should be made in its supplemental budget.
Furthermore, management should exert extra efforts to identify unreconciled book and bank balances as of December 2008 totaling negative P2,378,555.13 which remained unrecorded as of December 31, 2017.

Require the Municipal Accountant to apply necessary adjustments in order to avoid overstatements and/or understatements of accounts that have a substantial impact on the financial condition of the municipality, and submit to the Auditor a copy of the journal entry voucher made on the adjustment.

Require the recognition of the Road Networks of the Municipality as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 to fully reflect all its acquired asset.
Likewise, we enjoin all concern the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to work hand in hand to attain the objective of the Circular as soon as possible.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly report of bills rendered to market occupant which will be made as basis in the accrual and recording of rental income.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply accrual basis in recording monthly rental income as required under Par. 7, PPSAS 1.

We recommend that management hasten the completion and optimize the utilization of the fund by fast-tracking the project implementation and review those that may no longer be the utmost priority of the Municipality and reprogram the same to other more important PPAs economically beneficial to its constituents.

observations.managementAction: The BLGF issued Regional Memorandum Order No. 2017-003 dated Jan. 13, 2017 recommending the relief of the Municipal Treasurer effective at the close of office hours on Jan. 16, 2017
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer promised to deposit those unremitted collections as determined per cash examination.

observations.managementAction: We will correct the previous entries made based on the recommended adjusting entry in order not to overstate or understate the affected accounts. We will also coordinate with COA to resolve this matter for there are other accounts that will be overstated based on the adjusting entry made.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.managementAction: Henceforth, we will disallow any excess of the government share. Any budgetary provision in excess of the requirement should be reprogrammed accordingly.

We will inform the claimant about the lacking documents in their liquidations. We will also see to it next time that complete supporting documents are attached before submitting the same to your office.
The utilization is made pursuant to the provision of DECS-DBM-DILG JCC No. 1 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998.

We will correct wrong practices made by the municipality regarding this matter. We will see to it that the payroll maker will take necessary actions in order to prevent employees from having a net take home pay of less than P3,500.00.
observations.reason: Some employees cannot make a net take home pay of more than P3,500.00 due to loans of GSIS and other agency that should be required to be deducted monthly.

observations.managementAction: We will observe the provisions set under Sec. 18 of RA No. 9184.
The inventory team will continue in conducting physical inventory especially those items that were unserviceable.
observations.reason: The Accounting Department has now maintained PPE Ledger Cards for each accountable person. We’ve started recording these PPE cards from year 2016. The inventory team has not yet submitted the result of their physical inventory.

observations.managementAction: Most of the unliquidated cash advances were from calendar year 2000 in which majority of the persons liable were difficult to locate.
observations.reason: There was no liquidation report submitted by some accountable persons. Those accountable persons that were still employed in the LGU were deducted monthly through payroll/salary deduction.

observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan will look into and review the municipal revenue code for possible amendment of some of its provisions.
observations.reason: We still need revision to widen our resources.

observations.managementAction: The custody of all accountable forms is now lodged to the Assistant Municipal Treasurer per advice by the Bureau of Local Government Finance. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer already submitted some of the unaccounted form to the Office of the Municipal Auditor.
observations.reason: Partially accounted the income sources.

observations.managementAction: Management promised to consider the adoption of the recommendations relayed upon on all future projects of the municipality.
observations.reason: We only made tarpaulin to those big projects.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor promised to implement the recommendation at the soonest time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer has been relieved effective at the close of office hours of January 16, 2017, per BLGF- IX Regional Memorandum Order No. 2017-003 dated January 13, 2017. Further,

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to book-up reconciling items which can be properly supported with valid documents, and old reconciling items will be look into if the old accounting records can be located.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to look into the matter as long as the documents and other pertinent records are located.
observations.reason: For the old unreconciled balances, it remains the same for the records still cannot be located.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to exert effort in collecting the receivables of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Demand letters were already sent by the Municipal Treasurer and partial collections was implemented as evidence by receipt of payments from some creditors.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had already issued changes in the process in the issuance of fuel, in order to facilitate the timely preparation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report.
observations.reason: Liquidations were submitted to the accounting office however, it was not sufficient to cover all the advances.

observations.managementAction: Based on the reconciliation made by working it back, management found out that discrepancies and errors were due to erroneous recording of account code. Further verification shows that some accumulated amount were from previous records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant which was forwarded but left unreconciled. Necessary corrections were made to some accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant cannot reconcile the said items for some of the records were lost.

observations.managementAction: Accordingly, the negative balance of account honoraria was due to the failure of the Municipal Treasurer to remit the collection of sale of bidding documents. Based on the cash count made last December 2013, two (2) stubs of official receipt were not remitted.
observations.reason: The official receipts for bidding documents were still not declared.

observations.managementAction: The report of collections was made not in accordance of what is provided in Sec. 15 and 29 of NGAS due to lack of internal control of the Treasurer or Head of the Agency. The collectors remitted their collections, but the corresponding accountable forms were not properly declared. Only the total amount collected was reflected as collections and deposits, thus accumulating the amount of cash in vault to a bigger amount.
observations.reason: Reiterated under audit observation No. 2015-002

observations.managementAction: The LCE will issue memo to the Mun. Treasurer and the former Accountant to immediately exert efforts for the reconciliation between their offices.
The management is looking forward and making action to write to the Agency Head of the GPBB for exemption of the requirements EO No. 40. The LGU will avail internet connection.
observations.reason: The LGU had procured some common supplies. Recently, they already availed internet connections from the local subscriber.

observations.managementAction: The LCE will issue memo to the treasurer
observations.reason: The accountable Officers did not remit their collections daily. On the other hand, the liquidation of cash advances per verification was complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management consider the immediate relief of the Municipal Treasurer pending the final result of the cash examination.  Likewise, withhold payment of her salaries and other benefits until such time that all collections is accounted and deposited to the depository account of the Municipality.

Require the Municipal Accountant to properly record transactions in order to avoid overstatements and/or understatements of accounts that have a substantial impact on the financial condition of the municipality.
Likewise, the Municipal Accountant has to look into and effect the necessary adjustments enumerated to correct the Municipality’s financial records.

The Municipality should immediately stop the remittance of more than P100.00 per employee of the government share for HDMF contribution of its regular employees notwithstanding that the personal share of the HDMF contribution is more than the requirement.  Employees who may opt to reduce their contribution to only P100.00 per month may do so by giving a written authority to reduce their monthly contribution.
Henceforth, we will disallow any excess of the government share.  Any budgetary provision in excess of the requirement should be reprogrammed accordingly.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review the completeness of supporting documents in processing disbursement voucher, and not only to determine the completeness of requirements in order to prove the validity, propriety and legality of claims. In addition thereto, internal audit system has to be installed and maintained to deter occurrence of transactions with incomplete supporting documents.

We recommended that the LSB to refrain from providing for non-priority expenditures in the SEF budget, especially for those that are part of the regular activities of schools, and/or the DepEd. Instead, it should allocate SEF funds for the construction and repair of school buildings; facilities and equipment; educational research; acquisition of books and periodicals; and sports development. Also, the LSB should evaluate the programs/projects/activities to be included in the SEF budget, taking into consideration the objectives set forth in RA No. 7160 and RA No. 5447.

We recommended that the management shall see to it that deductions from payroll shall not result to a net take home pay of less than the amount mandated under the provision of the General Appropriations Act for a given year.
Likewise, management shall adopt a device that would immediately detect on the resulting net pay of the individual whenever loan application is made, and not to approve the application if the result thereof would reduce the net pay to below the statutory requirement.

We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions set under Section 18 of RA No. 9184. Relative thereto, every employee taking charge of the procurement process should see to it that every purchase request shall not bear the brand names of items to be procured.

We recommend that management instruct the Accounting and Property Offices to prepare/maintai n PPELCs/Property Cards and reconcile the same in order to determine the accuracy of the account balance as of year-end. Likewise, we recommend that the inventory committee has to complete the conduct of physical inventory-taking yearly to determine existence and condition of PPE. Furthermore, it is recommended that management retrieve all vouchers pertaining to the construction of the COA Office and Tribal Hall to effect proper recognition thereof. Also, management has to coordinate with the DSWD office to effect transfer of the Senior Citizen Building. On the other hand, coordinate with the PCSO and DILG to determine the cost of acquired vehicles donated by those agencies. In addition thereto, the GSO/disposal committee has to undertake immediate action to properly dispose of all unserviceable properties in storage to prevent them from further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: The inventory team will continue in conducting physical inventory especially those items that were unserviceab le. The GSO has now maintained PPE Ledger Cards for each accountable person. We’ve started recording these PPE cards from year 2016. The inventory team has not yet submitted the result of their physical inventory.
observations.reason: The Accounting Department has now maintained PPE Ledger Cards for each accountable person. We’ve started recording these PPE cards from year 2016. The inventory team has not yet submitted the result of their physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the AO’s concerned to immediately settle their overdue cash advances. As such, the Municipal Accountant in enjoined to send in demand letters to those concerned to once and for all ascertain the possibility of settlement thereof. The Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to closely monitor the compliance of the officials and employees concerned to the prescribed settlement period of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Most of the unliquidated cash advances were from calendar year 2000 in which majority of the persons liable were difficult to locate. Demand letters cannot be given to some of them for there was no exact address on file which resulted to poor operations.
observations.reason: There was no liquidation report submitted by some accountable persons. Those accountable persons that were still employed in the LGU were deducted monthly through payroll/salar y deduction.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipality considers the following: Review personnel requirement of Treasurer’s Office to implement the code including the provision on License Inspector; Device the sworn statement form to facilitate compliance of the taxpayer on the declaration of revenue in obtaining business permit and residence certificate; Review certain provisions especially on Cart or Sledge Registration Fee; Dog License Fee; Permit Fee on Agricultural Machinery and Other Heavy Equipment; Permit fee for the Conduct of Group Activities; Fighting Cock License Fee; Permit fee on Profession not requiring Government Examination; and Mayor’s Clearance to transport and ship various animals and goods; Adopt proper and clear procedure on how to implement certain provision of the code; and Review the policy on slaughter fee in the absence of a Municipal Slaughterhouse.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan will look into and review the municipal revenue code for possible amendment of some of its provisions.
observations.reason: We still need revision to widen our resources.

observations.recommendation: We suggest that equivalent or similar measure as discussed above be considered in order to improve the process of monitoring and release of accountable forms to avoid the occurrence of the same incident in the future. The Municipal Accountant is most likely the person to maintain a separate monitoring and gives authority for its succeeding release. Likewise, require the regular preparation and submission of the RAAF.
observations.managementAction: The custody of all accountable forms is now lodged to the Assistant Municipal Treasurer per advice by the Bureau of Local Government Finance. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer already submitted some of the unaccounted accountable form to the Office of the Municipal Auditor.
observations.reason: Partially accounted the income sources.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality limits its expenditure for Personnel Services within the limitation set forth under Section 325(a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The appropriati on for PS during the preparation of Annual Budget Circular for FY 2015 exceeded the percentage limitation pegged by the Local Government Code on account of the requirement of providing the total compensation of the working force of the LGU pursuant to LBC No. 99 and providing funds for the Magna Carta Benefit of Public Health Workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to the prescribed guidelines set out under Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 in relation to the rules and regulations cited under the National Internal Revenue Code of 1997 concerning the deduction, withholding and remittance of taxes due to the national government.
observations.managementAction: We will withhold income taxes for all the benefits received from the PhilHealth – Capitation Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that travel orders authorizing the travel of municipal officials and employees including PNP personnel shall be issued and/or approved by the local chief executive as provided for under Section 96 of RA No. 7160. We further recommend that the claim for actual travel expenses must be supported with the certification issued by the local chief executive rendering such as absolutely necessary in the performance of an assignment as mandated by EO No. 298. The Municipal Accountant is advised to see to it that only the allowed reimbursement of per diem must be approved taking into account the specific percentage of entitlement cited under EO No. 298. Also, she has to see to it that all minimum required documentary requirements are complied with before approval of the voucher. Lastly, we reiterate the recommendations reached last year that the Municipal Budget Officer has to classify expense account as prescribed, at the same time require the Municipal Accountant to take up the transaction as required under the Chart of Accounts and advised the former for its proper classification.
observations.managementAction: We will no longer allow disbursements of travel without the approved Travel Order from the local chief executive. Actual travel expenses were supported with certificati on or approval by the local chief executive that they were authorized to claim actual expenses. The approval of per diem was based in EO No. 298 computation. Registration fee were separately classified as training expenses and no longer part of the travelling expenses. The Municipal Accountant is advised to see to it that the allowed reimbursement of per diem must be approved taking into account the specific percentage of entitlement cited under EO No. 298. Also, she has to see to it that all minimum required documentary requirements are complied with before approval of the voucher. Lastly, we reiterate the recommendations reached last year that the Municipal Budget Officer has to classify expense account as prescribed, at the same time require the Municipal Accountant to take up the transaction as required under the Chart of Accounts and advised the former for its proper classification.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that concerned Department Heads submit all the required financial reports and records within the deadlines as discussed.
observations.managementAction: During the 1st quarter of the year, monthly submission of trial balance was delayed due to closing of the year end trial balance. Vouchers were not submitted on time because some of it lack signatures and others did not have official receipts attached when submitted from the treasury department.
observations.reason: Monthly trial balance was submitted on or before the 20th day of the following month per NGAS Manual. BRS for LGU was also submitted within 5 days from receipt of bank statement. However, for vouchers, we will communicate with the treasury for the improvement of our operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the amount intended for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) should be allocated and expended within the guidelines set forth under the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 issued by the DILG, DBM, and the NDRRMC.
observations.managementAction: The appropriati on for maintenance of patrol car as reflected in our budget for LDRRMF were augmented to maintenance of rescue vehicle and were disbursed for the maintenance of such vehicle while the maintenance of patrol car were charged to the Office of the Municipal Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to the requirements of RA 9003 in its approach of protecting the environment. Likewise, improve its practice in treating its personnel directly garbage disposal activity by providing them complete protective sanitation tools and equipment and consider giving additional financial reward like sharing in the proceeds of the sale of recycled materials.
observations.managementAction: We will consider the adoption of the recommenda tion relayed during the exit conference. It also plans to consider all other programs implemented by other LGUs of the province in relation to environment protection.
observations.reason: We already made the Solid Waste Management Plan, Establishment of Soil Enhancer and Establishment of Barangay MRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GFPS should not only be constituted but should be functioning by following the guidelines set forth under PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 and the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 issued and signed by the PCW, DILG, DBM, and the NEDA. Likewise to improve its understanding and as part of improving awareness on GAD, an in-house seminars and agency sponsored symposium maybe considered.
observations.managementAction: We commit to take corrective actions and to adhere to existing rules and regulations based on the recommendat ion.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines set under RA No. 7160 in relation to the utilization of the Special Education Fund. We recommend further that the Municipal Budget Officer assign all Obligation Requests with numbers in accordance with the prescribed codes before certifying to the existence of appropriation as provided under Sec. 89 Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: We promised to adhere to the recommendat ion relayed during the exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the wages of job order worker assigned to the Municipal Cemetery will not be charged to the 20% EDF and the appropriation for Project Counterpart Fund for Sangguniang Bayan Members be identified first at the level of the Local Development Council and not during its implementation, and the remaining balance thereof shall be reprogrammed accordingly.
observations.managementAction: We commit to corrective actions and to adhere to existing rules and regulations based on the recommendation thereat effective upon the issuance of AOM specificall y the wages of job order worker assigned in the municipal cemetery whose task was to identify the specific area of burial to be charged to the GF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the information and publicity requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004 with regards to future programs/projects/activities to be implemented.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to consider the adoption of the recommendat ions relayed upon on all future projects of the municipality.
observations.reason: We only made tarpaulin to those big projects.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections until February 28, 2015. Meanwhile, relieve her of further handling cash collections until such time that all previous collections under her custody is deposited to the depository account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor promised to implement the recommendation at the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant should properly effect the necessary adjustments. Charges which are indirect disbursements should be properly covered with Obligation Request before this can be taken up in the books of account. On the other hand, all valid outstanding checks in which checks were already staled or beyond the six (6) month period, should be adjusted and recorded as payable of the Municipality. Furthermore, unidentified reconciling items at the beginning of the period should be look into.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to book-up reconciling items which can be properly supported with valid documents, and old reconciling items will be look into if the old accounting records can be located.
observations.reason: For the old unreconciled balances, it remains the same for the records still cannot be located.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned officers to explain the circumstances on the existence of the account, and immediate restitution of their accountability should be made. On the other hand the unidentified employees with accountability of P191,303.42 should be determined by analyzing the transaction and retrieve the originating documents, in order to pinpoint responsibility thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant promised to look into the matter as long as the documents and other pertinent records are located.
observations.reason: Latest collections from Revenue Collectors were remitted and submitted to the accounting office. However, for the old receipts that were in the Treasurer’s Office remain unremitted until present. The accountant and the designated liquidating officer will now reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation: The Municipality should pay the BAC Honoraria based on the proceeds of the sale of the bidding documents. In cases of deficiency in collections from procurement activities and non availability of agency savings, the amount of honoraria shall be adjusted proportionately for all those entitled thereto. Also, strictly follow the rates indicated in the guide in the audit of the Grant of BAC Honoraria, otherwise payment exceeding the rates allowed and the payment exceeding the proceeds of the sale bid documents will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Accordingly, the negative balance of account honoraria was due to the failure of the Municipal Treasurer to remit the collection of sale of bidding documents. Based on the cash count made last December 2013, two (2) stubs of official receipt were not remitted.
observations.reason: The official receipts for bidding documents were still not declared.

observations.recommendation: 1. Require the Treasurer and Revenue Collectors to remit collections daily and intact and to observe proper rules and regulations in the recording, remittance and deposits to ensure that government funds are accounted for. 2. Require the issuance of Official Receipts in correct numerical sequence and closely monitor the correct balances of the Report of Accountable Forms. 3. Require the Treasurer to properly and correctly prepare reports of collections and deposits, and report of accountability for accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: The report of collections was made not in accordance of what is provided in Sec. 15 and 29 of NGAS due to lack of internal control of the Treasurer or Head of the Agency. The collectors remitted their collections, but the corresponding accountable forms were not properly declared. Only the total amount collected was reflected as collections and deposits, thus accumulating the amount of cash in vault to a bigger amount.
observations.reason: Reiterated under audit observation No. 2015-002

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer and all collectors to strictly observe the rules and regulations in the proper custody of collections in the matter of recording, remittance and deposits to ensure that government funds are properly accounted for and the risk of misappropriation is avoided. The Municipal Treasurer should closely monitor the remittances of collectors and verify their Report of Collections and Deposits, check the Statement of Accountable Forms and make physical count of the Accountable Forms in the custody of the collectors to ensure that the collector’s accountability was fully accounted for. Lastly, the Municipal Treasurer should exert efforts to cause the reconciliation of the treasury and accounting records of the cash in vault account to eliminate the doubt of misappropriatio n of funds.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will issue memo to Mun. Treasurer and the former Accountant to immediately exert efforts for the reconciliation of records between their offices.

observations.recommendation: Require the Supply Office to procure the common supplies of the LGU from the Procurement Service of the DBM, pursuant to Executive Order No. 40 as reiterated under Administrative Order No. 17.
observations.managementAction: The management is looking forward and making action to write to the Agency Head of the GPBB for exemption of the requirement s EO No. 40. The LGU will avail internet connection and were already appropriate d.
observations.reason: The LGU had procured some common supplies. Recently, they already availed internet connections from the local subscriber.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor should initiate the institution of administrative or criminal proceedings against erring public officials and employees of the LGU. Moreover, require those concerned to immediately liquidate/settle outstanding cash advances and direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections as provided by existing laws, rules, and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE will issue memo to the treasurer and the former Accountant. The accountable Officers did not remit their collections daily. On the other hand, the liquidation of cash advances per verification was complied with.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to issue demand letters and exert extra effort in collecting all the receivables of the Municipality. Amount receivable from deceased individuals, if any, should be reviewed as to its collectability, if determined to be no longer collectible, request from the Commission of Audit for the dropping of the amount from the books of account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to exert effort in collecting the receivables of the Municipality. Amount receivable from deceased individuals, if any, should be reviewed as to its collectability, if determined to be no longer collectible, request from the Commission of Audit for the dropping of the amount from the books of account of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Demands letters were already sent by the Municipal Treasurer and partial collections was implemented as evidence by receipt of payments from some creditors.

observations.recommendation: Require the Revenue Collectors, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant, to coordinate their actions and to observe the rules and regulations in handling collections as to its remittance, deposits, and recording to ensure that income are properly accounted for on its proper period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to adopt the recommendat ion.
observations.reason: Latest collections from Revenue Collectors were remitted and submitted to the accounting office. However, for the old receipts that were in the Treasurer’s Office remain unremitted until present. The accountant and the designated liquidating officer will now reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GSO should comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulations on fuel consumption and submit the report to the Auditor for verification. Likewise, the process of documentation of fuel utilization should be reviewed and make any changes to improve the system in order to facilitate the preparation of the monthly Fuel Consumption Report. Further, all personnel concerned, including all drivers of the LGU should be made aware of the proper procedure and the necessity of having a timely and regular report of fuel consumption. Non-compliance with said regulations may cause the suspension and/or disallowance of the voucher for the payment of gas/fuel for the LGU’s motor vehicles and the withholding of salary of personnel concerned until the required reports shall have been submitted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor had already issued changes in the process in the issuance of fuel, in order to facilitate the timely preparation of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report.
observations.reason: Liquidations were submitted to the accounting office however, it was not sufficient to cover all the advances.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to verify the above and make the necessary corrections to have an accurate report on its liability account.
observations.managementAction: Based on the reconcilia tion made by working it back, management found out that discrepanci es and errors were due to erroneous recording of account code entries. Further verificatio n shows that some accumulated amount were from previous records of the OIC-Municipal Accountant which was forwarded but left unreconcile d. Necessary corrections were made to some accounts.
observations.reason: The OIC – Municipal Accountant cannot reconcile the said items for some of the records were lost.

observations.recommendation: Require the Revenue Collectors, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant, to coordinate their actions and to observe the rules and regulations in handling collections as to its remittance, deposits, and recording to ensure that income are properly accounted for on its proper period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to adopt the recommendat ion.
observations.reason: Latest collections from Revenue Collectors were remitted and submitted to the accounting office. However, for the old receipts that were in the Treasurer’s Office remain unremitted until present. The accountant and the designated liquidating officer will now reconcile their records.
